Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
About Us 🚀

Hostelworld is the world's leading Social Travel platform - a social network-powered OTA on a mission to help travellers find their people and create unforgettable moments together. Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Ireland, we're a team of around 270 people serving hostel partners across more than 180 countries.

Since launching our social network in 2022, we've welcomed over 3.4 million social members who aren't just booking trips, they're messaging, finding events, meeting up and becoming powerful brand advocates. This community generates a rich proprietary dataset of 16 million+ chat messages and 17 million bookings, powering AI-driven personalisation and network effects that no generalist OTA can replicate.

We are not just another OTA - we created an entirely new category – Social Travel. If you want to shape the future of travel by putting people and shared experiences at the heart of it, you'll fit right in here - Learn more about our Team!

Our Culture

At Hostelworld, our culture is a direct reflection of our customers: adventurous, curious, and social. We have a shared love of travel that fuels our work and connects us on a deeper level. It’s a fast-paced environment that blends the agility and "scrappy" resourcefulness of a start-up with the experience and ambition of a global, publicly listed company.

We are a team of pragmatic optimists who are data-obsessed and results-driven. We value a ‘test and learn’ mindset, encouraging experimentation and empowering our teams to take calculated risks. We believe in doing the right thing—for our customers, our partners, and each other. We foster a supportive and collaborative atmosphere where diverse perspectives are celebrated, and where every team member has the opportunity to make a significant impact on our journey. We embrace the journey, not just the destination, and we seek leaders who will thrive in a dynamic environment and inspire their teams to do the same.

LOCATION🌍

This role is based in Portugal. We have an office hub in Porto available for those who prefer a hybrid model where you can spend time with colleagues in-person. You will need to be able to commute to our office hub as required from time to time.

Who You'll Work With 👨🏽‍🤝‍👨🏼

The Marketing Analytics team is the data engine behind paid marketing decisions at Hostelworld. We own marketing measurement & experimentation, run incrementality experiments, build elasticity and budget-optimisation models, and own the dbt models and Tableau dashboards that Performance Marketing relies on.

The team sits inside the broader Analytics & Insights function and partners with Performance Marketing, Brand, Product, CRM, and Finance.

The role

Role

We are hiring a Marketing Data Analyst to join the team. This is a hands-on individual-contributor role combining four skill blocks:


Analysis — turning commercial questions into reproducible analyses that influence budget, bidding, and channel decisions.
Commercial & business focus — translating findings into narratives, opportunity sizings, and recommendations stakeholders can act on.
Analytics engineering (data modelling & dbt) — building and maintaining dimensional dbt models in Snowflake to power downstream reporting and analyses.
Tableau reporting — designing, optimising, and governing the dashboards used across Marketing and senior leadership audiences.


You will own a portfolio of recurring Tableau reports, dbt data models, analyses, and projects around marketing attribution, measurement, experimentation, optimisation & customer modelling.

What You'll Do 👩‍💻


Analysis
Run weekly and monthly performance deep-dives across acquisition channels (PPC, Paid Social, Paid App, Affiliates).
Build and maintain channel-level pacing reports, business reviews, and YoY trend analyses.
Investigate anomalies (cost spikes, conversion drops, attribution shifts) and deliver root-cause analysis.
Support measurement initiatives: incrementality tests, A/B tests, cohort and CLV analyses.
Produce clear, reproducible insight reports with charts, tables, and explicit recommendations.
Commercial & business focus
Size investment opportunities and contribute to budget-allocation business cases.
Support quarterly investment-opportunity memos and roadmap planning.
Maintain fluency in unit economics: CAC, CLV, payback, contribution margin as the enterprise north-star metrics.
Translate analyses into narratives for non-technical stakeholders.
Surface gaps proactively: flag missing topics, broken assumptions, and untapped opportunities.
Analytics engineering (data modelling & dbt)
Design and maintain dimensional dbt models in Snowflake.
Add and maintain dbt tests on critical models.
Refactor legacy SQL and migrate Tableau-embedded calculations upstream into governed dbt marts.
Follow Git workflows, peer review, and modular SQL practices.
Partner with Analytics Engineering on lineage clean-ups, incremental patterns, and ingestion of new marketing-platform sources.
Tableau reporting
Build and own Tableau dashboards used by Performance Marketing, Brand and Commercial teams.
Optimise workbook performance.
Migrate complex calculations from Tableau into upstream dbt models to keep workbooks thin.
Maintain a clean metric layer between dbt and Tableau so definitions are consistent.


What We’re Looking For 👀

Must-have


2–3 years of commercial analytics experience, ideally in marketing, e-commerce, or consumer-tech contexts.
Advanced SQL — window functions, CTEs, performance-aware queries on cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift).
dbt — production experience building models, writing tests, and managing lineage and documentation.
Tableau — production experience designing and optimising dashboards used by real stakeholders.
Python for analytics — comfortable in Jupyter with pandas and a plotting library (plotly, matplotlib, or similar); able to write reproducible scripts.
Commercial fluency — comfortable with CAC, CLV, payback, contribution margin; able to translate a number into a recommendation.
Stakeholder communication — clear written narratives, structured insight reports, opinionated recommendations.
Software-engineering best practices — Git workflows, peer review, modular code, parameterised queries.


Nice-to-have


Exposure to marketing attribution (multi-touch, data-driven, MMM, incrementality testing).
Hands-on experience with paid-media platforms (Google Ads, Meta Ads, TikTok Ads, GA4,AppsFlyer).
Experience with Streamlit or other analytical-app frameworks.
Familiarity with Airflow or another orchestration tool.
Awareness of mobile measurement (SKAdNetwork / SKAN, Firebase, AppsFlyer).
Exposure to causal inference methods (synthetic control, CausalImpact, difference-in-differences).


What We Offer 💯


Competitive salary & benefits
Enhanced annual leave plus 3 Wellbeing Days per year
Paid family leave (maternity, paternity, surrogacy & adoption)
Agile working (plus a Working from Abroad Policy!)
Support for your ongoing growth & development
Inclusive people policies (sickness, menopause, compassionate and fertility leave)
A chance to give back to your local community with 5 paid volunteering days


OUR BEHAVIOURS 🏆


Grow others - We fundamentally believe that investing in growing others benefits everyone, whether it's helping them develop hard or soft skills. We want learning and growing to be part of our DNA to help makes us a better team, together.
Master it - We are obsessed with our area of expertise and enjoy developing our skills. We rarely take things at face value; we investigate, interrogate, and always look for ‘the why,’ and wherever possible, we use data to find the best solution.
Collaborate - We are in it together, for the tough stuff and the celebrations too. To achieve the best results, we need expertise from all areas of the organisation, and we wholeheartedly welcome diverse thinking.
Adapt - We work fluidly, adapting to new information and the evolving environment while staying committed to our goals. Innovation and experimentation fuel our projects and we’re never afraid to pivot.
Deliver - Our focus is always on the end result; we value outcomes over activity. We collaborate to deliver work at speed without dropping any of our other behaviours.


We believe in talented and diverse teams that reflect the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we operate. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences. We lay out the above requirements to guide applicants to the experiences that we believe will allow you to be successful in the role. If you don’t meet them all, please consider applying if you think you can still perform the role as described.

Hostelworld is the world’s leading Social Travel platform - a social network powered OTA built to help travellers find their people.

Our mission is simple: we connect like‑minded travellers so they can create unforgettable moments together. Most people go hostelling to meet others, and we’ve built a global ecosystem that makes those connections happen.

Since launching our social network in 2022, we’ve welcomed more than 2.6 million social members who aren’t just booking trips — they’re messaging, meeting up, sharing stories and becoming passionate advocates for our brand. This vibrant community generates rich, authentic content and powerful social proof that fuels our growth.

Our focus on helping travellers find people to hang out with has created a new category in travel. We’re not a traditional OTA: our mobile‑native social features create a sticky, high‑engagement experience, and the proprietary data generated by our community gives us a competitive advantage that is extremely hard to replicate. This “social flywheel” helps us understand traveller behaviour, personalise experiences and deliver a differentiated product that keeps users coming back.

Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Ireland, we are approximately 270 employees strong, with hostel partners in more than 180 countries. Alongside our focus on connection, we are committed to building a better world — from advancing sustainability in the hostelling sector to giving travellers the tools to make more informed and climate‑conscious choices.

At Hostelworld, we’re shaping the future of travel by putting people, community and shared experiences at the heart of everything we do.

Hostelworld is the world's leading Social Travel platform - a social network-powered OTA on a mission to help travellers find their people and create unforgettable moments together. Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Ireland, we're a team of around 270 people serving hostel partners across more than 180 countries.

Since launching our social network in 2022, we've welcomed over 3.4 million social members who aren't just booking trips, they're messaging, finding events, meeting up and becoming powerful brand advocates. This community generates a rich proprietary dataset of 16 million+ chat messages and 17 million bookings, powering AI-driven personalisation and network effects that no generalist OTA can replicate.

We are not just another OTA - we created an entirely new category – Social Travel. If you want to shape the future of travel by putting people and shared experiences at the heart of it, you'll fit right in here - Learn more about our Team!

Our Culture

At Hostelworld, our culture is a direct reflection of our customers: adventurous, curious, and social. We have a shared love of travel that fuels our work and connects us on a deeper level. It’s a fast-paced environment that blends the agility and "scrappy" resourcefulness of a start-up with the experience and ambition of a global, publicly listed company.

We are a team of pragmatic optimists who are data-obsessed and results-driven. We value a ‘test and learn’ mindset, encouraging experimentation and empowering our teams to take calculated risks. We believe in doing the right thing—for our customers, our partners, and each other. We foster a supportive and collaborative atmosphere where diverse perspectives are celebrated, and where every team member has the opportunity to make a significant impact on our journey. We embrace the journey, not just the destination, and we seek leaders who will thrive in a dynamic environment and inspire their teams to do the same.

LOCATION🌍

This role is based in Portugal. We have an office hub in Porto available for those who prefer a hybrid model where you can spend time with colleagues in-person. You will need to be able to commute to our office hub as required from time to time.

Who You'll Work With 👨🏽‍🤝‍👨🏼

The Marketing Analytics team is the data engine behind paid marketing decisions at Hostelworld. We own marketing measurement & experimentation, run incrementality experiments, build elasticity and budget-optimisation models, and own the dbt models and Tableau dashboards that Performance Marketing relies on.

The team sits inside the broader Analytics & Insights function and partners with Performance Marketing, Brand, Product, CRM, and Finance.

The role

Role

We are hiring a Marketing Data Analyst to join the team. This is a hands-on individual-contributor role combining four skill blocks:

  • Analysis — turning commercial questions into reproducible analyses that influence budget, bidding, and channel decisions.
  • Commercial & business focus — translating findings into narratives, opportunity sizings, and recommendations stakeholders can act on.
  • Analytics engineering (data modelling & dbt) — building and maintaining dimensional dbt models in Snowflake to power downstream reporting and analyses.
  • Tableau reporting — designing, optimising, and governing the dashboards used across Marketing and senior leadership audiences.

You will own a portfolio of recurring Tableau reports, dbt data models, analyses, and projects around marketing attribution, measurement, experimentation, optimisation & customer modelling.

What You'll Do 👩‍💻

  • Analysis
  • Run weekly and monthly performance deep-dives across acquisition channels (PPC, Paid Social, Paid App, Affiliates).
  • Build and maintain channel-level pacing reports, business reviews, and YoY trend analyses.
  • Investigate anomalies (cost spikes, conversion drops, attribution shifts) and deliver root-cause analysis.
  • Support measurement initiatives: incrementality tests, A/B tests, cohort and CLV analyses.
  • Produce clear, reproducible insight reports with charts, tables, and explicit recommendations.
  • Commercial & business focus
  • Size investment opportunities and contribute to budget-allocation business cases.
  • Support quarterly investment-opportunity memos and roadmap planning.
  • Maintain fluency in unit economics: CAC, CLV, payback, contribution margin as the enterprise north-star metrics.
  • Translate analyses into narratives for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Surface gaps proactively: flag missing topics, broken assumptions, and untapped opportunities.
  • Analytics engineering (data modelling & dbt)
  • Design and maintain dimensional dbt models in Snowflake.
  • Add and maintain dbt tests on critical models.
  • Refactor legacy SQL and migrate Tableau-embedded calculations upstream into governed dbt marts.
  • Follow Git workflows, peer review, and modular SQL practices.
  • Partner with Analytics Engineering on lineage clean-ups, incremental patterns, and ingestion of new marketing-platform sources.
  • Tableau reporting
  • Build and own Tableau dashboards used by Performance Marketing, Brand and Commercial teams.
  • Optimise workbook performance.
  • Migrate complex calculations from Tableau into upstream dbt models to keep workbooks thin.
  • Maintain a clean metric layer between dbt and Tableau so definitions are consistent.

What We’re Looking For 👀

Must-have

  • 2–3 years of commercial analytics experience, ideally in marketing, e-commerce, or consumer-tech contexts.
  • Advanced SQL — window functions, CTEs, performance-aware queries on cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift).
  • dbt — production experience building models, writing tests, and managing lineage and documentation.
  • Tableau — production experience designing and optimising dashboards used by real stakeholders.
  • Python for analytics — comfortable in Jupyter with pandas and a plotting library (plotly, matplotlib, or similar); able to write reproducible scripts.
  • Commercial fluency — comfortable with CAC, CLV, payback, contribution margin; able to translate a number into a recommendation.
  • Stakeholder communication — clear written narratives, structured insight reports, opinionated recommendations.
  • Software-engineering best practices — Git workflows, peer review, modular code, parameterised queries.

Nice-to-have

  • Exposure to marketing attribution (multi-touch, data-driven, MMM, incrementality testing).
  • Hands-on experience with paid-media platforms (Google Ads, Meta Ads, TikTok Ads, GA4,AppsFlyer).
  • Experience with Streamlit or other analytical-app frameworks.
  • Familiarity with Airflow or another orchestration tool.
  • Awareness of mobile measurement (SKAdNetwork / SKAN, Firebase, AppsFlyer).
  • Exposure to causal inference methods (synthetic control, CausalImpact, difference-in-differences).

What We Offer 💯

  • Competitive salary & benefits
  • Enhanced annual leave plus 3 Wellbeing Days per year
  • Paid family leave (maternity, paternity, surrogacy & adoption)
  • Agile working (plus a Working from Abroad Policy!)
  • Support for your ongoing growth & development
  • Inclusive people policies (sickness, menopause, compassionate and fertility leave)
  • A chance to give back to your local community with 5 paid volunteering days

OUR BEHAVIOURS 🏆

  • Grow others - We fundamentally believe that investing in growing others benefits everyone, whether it's helping them develop hard or soft skills. We want learning and growing to be part of our DNA to help makes us a better team, together.
  • Master it - We are obsessed with our area of expertise and enjoy developing our skills. We rarely take things at face value; we investigate, interrogate, and always look for ‘the why,’ and wherever possible, we use data to find the best solution.
  • Collaborate - We are in it together, for the tough stuff and the celebrations too. To achieve the best results, we need expertise from all areas of the organisation, and we wholeheartedly welcome diverse thinking.
  • Adapt - We work fluidly, adapting to new information and the evolving environment while staying committed to our goals. Innovation and experimentation fuel our projects and we’re never afraid to pivot.
  • Deliver - Our focus is always on the end result; we value outcomes over activity. We collaborate to deliver work at speed without dropping any of our other behaviours.

We believe in talented and diverse teams that reflect the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we operate. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences. We lay out the above requirements to guide applicants to the experiences that we believe will allow you to be successful in the role. If you don’t meet them all, please consider applying if you think you can still perform the role as described.

Hostelworld is the world’s leading Social Travel platform - a social network powered OTA built to help travellers find their people.

Our mission is simple: we connect like‑minded travellers so they can create unforgettable moments together. Most people go hostelling to meet others, and we’ve built a global ecosystem that makes those connections happen.

Since launching our social network in 2022, we’ve welcomed more than 2.6 million social members who aren’t just booking trips — they’re messaging, meeting up, sharing stories and becoming passionate advocates for our brand. This vibrant community generates rich, authentic content and powerful social proof that fuels our growth.

Our focus on helping travellers find people to hang out with has created a new category in travel. We’re not a traditional OTA: our mobile‑native social features create a sticky, high‑engagement experience, and the proprietary data generated by our community gives us a competitive advantage that is extremely hard to replicate. This “social flywheel” helps us understand traveller behaviour, personalise experiences and deliver a differentiated product that keeps users coming back.

Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Ireland, we are approximately 270 employees strong, with hostel partners in more than 180 countries. Alongside our focus on connection, we are committed to building a better world — from advancing sustainability in the hostelling sector to giving travellers the tools to make more informed and climate‑conscious choices.

At Hostelworld, we’re shaping the future of travel by putting people, community and shared experiences at the heart of everything we do.

Title: Data Analyst (f/m/div.)
Description:
The Bosch group has more than 400 000 employees around the world, present in 60 countries, and we are proud to impact people’s lives and to work towards a more sustainable future.



Bosch Service Solutions in Lisbon develops and offers Customer Experience, Mobility, and Monitoring solutions for its clients, and plays a prominent role in providing Engineering and Software Development services in the technology market. Every day, our teams find fast, efficient, and innovative solutions for thousands of people.



At Bosch, we shape the future by inventing high-quality technologies and services that spark enthusiasm and enrich people’s lives. Our promise to our associates is rock-solid: we grow together, we enjoy our work, and we inspire each other. Join in and feel the difference in mindsets, cultures, generations, identities and perspectives. Everyone should bring their authenticity and work together respectfully. Bosch is an employer that values diversity and equal opportunities. We welcome applications from people with disabilities and we can provide reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process and in the performance of professional activity. By including everyone and ensuring equal opportunities we unleash our full potential.





Job Description


Analyze historical data to identify trends, anomalies, and performance drivers
Translate data-driven insights into actionable recommendations to support diagnostics and business decisions for product development
Design and build practical algorithms based on data thresholds and business heuristics to support decision-making
Provide dedicated data-driven support for Engineering Development, including punctual analysis and defining data requirements
Analyze customer ticket data to identify patterns and opportunities for product improvement
Collaborate closely with software engineers, product managers, and other stakeholders to embed data insights into the development lifecycle
Create and present compelling reports and data stories to communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical audience
Work closely with software engineers, product managers, and data scientists, data engineers and hardware engineers


Qualifications


Highly proficient in Python and its data analysis libraries, including Pandas, Matplotlib, and PySpark. Basic proficiency in Bash/Linux commands.
Possesses a strong foundation in descriptive analytics, with demonstrated critical thinking and problem-solving capabilities
Capable of working effectively with both structured and unstructured data.
Writes clean, maintainable code and consistently applies version control (Git) as a standard practice within the workflow.
Capable of working effectively with both structured and unstructured data.
Knowledge of dashboard visualization tools, such as Streamlit, would be considered an advantage.
Demonstrates strong data storytelling abilities and delivers clear, compelling reports and presentations tailored to diverse audiences.


Additional Information



Work means:



⚖️ Flexible work conditions



🔀 Hybrid work system



🌐 Exchange with colleagues around the world



🧑‍⚕️ Health insurance and medical office on site



📚 Training opportunities



📈 Opportunities for career progression and continuous professional development



💲 Access to great discounts in partnerships and Bosch products



🏋️ Sports and health related activities



🚉 Great access to public transports



🅿️ Free parking lot



🍽️ Canteen







Success stories don´t just happen. They are made...



Make it happen! We are looking forward to your application!

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

PEOPLE TEAM

The People team plays a pivotal role in supporting the business by strategically overseeing the complete employee journey. From drawing in top talent and fostering a high-performance culture and career development, they are instrumental in creating a positive and inclusive workplace environment and managing our workspaces.

PORTO

Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

ROLE

As part of the People Data & Insights team, you will be a key player in our mission to shape our organizational strategy and empower the business with data-driven insights. We are the analytical and strategic engine that enables the People Team and senior leadership to make smarter, more strategic decisions about our organization and our people. This role will provide you with a deep understanding of the intersection of People Analytics, business strategy, organizational transformation, and process automation. You will be at the center of critical initiatives that drive our long-term vision and operational efficiency.

What You'll Do


Provide Strategic Analysis: Be passionate about optimization and critical thinking. You will analyze complex headcount data and people-related financial numbers, providing key insights for forecasting, budgeting, and identifying efficiency opportunities
Collaborate Across the Business: Liaise with multiple internal and external stakeholders globally, ensuring strategic analysis and project updates are channelled effectively through the business
Champion Process Automation: Identify and analyze existing People processes, designing clean, automated, and scalable solutions, using the most recent tech capabilities (AI-driven) that flow smoothly and unlock more time for high-value work
Transform Data into Actionable Insights: Developing dashboards and reports ensuring their correct communication to all involved stakeholders. You will transform complex data into clear, actionable insights for leadership
Leverage Our HR Tech Stack: Maximize the potential of our core HRIS, Workday, by building and enhancing its native dashboards and reports. Your focus will be on creating intuitive, self-service analytics tools that empower our stakeholders with on-demand data to drive their decisions
Drive Strategic & Transformational Projects: Be a key player in multi-disciplinary, end-to-end project teams to address new business needs, driving continuous improvement and strategic change initiatives related to our people and processes





Who You Are


Degree in Economics, Management, Mathematics, Engineering or other relevant quantitative degree
2-3 years of professional experience mandatory
Fluent English is mandatory
Proficiency in Excel and Google Sheets is mandatory
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI is a strong plus
Familiarity with BigQuery is a plus
Familiarity with AI solutions is a plus
Proven experience in a similar role blending data analysis, project management, and strategic thinking
Strong numerical reasoning, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Organized and detail-oriented with a proactive approach to managing projects end-to-end





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities and free access to Udemy
Flexible benefits program





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT



Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.





SCAM DISCLAIMER



It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent Farfetch in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, Farfetch will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process.





As part of the People Data & Insights team, you will be a key player in our mission to shape our organizational strategy and empower the business with data-driven insights. We are the analytical and strategic engine that enables the People Team and senior leadership to make smarter, more strategic decisions about our organization and our people.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Power BI Developer – Front End Focus
Description:
As a global leader in industrial storage solutions, stow Group is renowned for its engineering excellence and commitment to innovation. With over 45 years of industry expertise, we design and implement powerful racking and automation systems for clients worldwide. stow Group has two independent brands under its roof: stow for racking & semi-automated solutions and Movu Robotics for warehouse automation.

Headquartered in Belgium, stow Group has production sites, affiliates and sales offices across all of Europe & USA, including a new racking production plant in USA as of April 2026. stow’s activity in Portugal began in 2019 with a production site and sales office in Ovar.

Location:

Ovar

, Portugal

Job Description

As a Power BI Developer, you are the driving force behind our data visualisation and front-end reporting environment. You translate complex business questions into clear, user-friendly dashboards and semantic models that support better decisions. Your core strengths lie on the frontend: Power BI design, DAX expertise, and stakeholder engagement. Experience with Microsoft Fabric however, will be considered a major asset.

Tasks & Responsibilities

BI Development & Analytics Delivery


Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service Power BI solutions aligned with business requirements.
Translate business questions into KPI definitions, metrics, and clear visual storytelling; validate outcomes with stakeholders.
Build and optimise semantic models (star schema, dimensional modelling) for consistent, high-performance reporting.
Ensure reliability and usability of BI solutions through model optimisation, refresh monitoring, incident follow-up, and continuous improvement.
Write advanced DAX formulas and M queries in Power Query for complex calculations and data transformations.



Data Management (Modelling, Integration & Quality)


Build and monitor data integration pipelines (ETL/ELT), ensuring secure and reliable data delivery from source to reporting layer.
Implement data quality controls (validation rules, reconciliations, error handling) and contribute to master data consistency.
Maintain accurate metadata and technical documentation (data lineage, definitions, refresh cycles) to keep data auditable and trustworthy.



Stakeholder Engagement


Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to refine requirements and ensure successful adoption of BI products.
Communicate data logic clearly — KPI definitions, lineage, and assumptions — so decision-makers can act with confidence.
Apply data governance practices: naming conventions, documentation standards, access principles, and retention rules.



Technology & Continuous Improvement


Stay up to date with BI and data management technologies and propose practical improvements to architecture, tooling, and ways of working.
Contribute to proof-of-concepts and adoption of new BI capabilities, ensuring maintainability, security, and cost-awareness.



Required

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree or higher in ICT, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or equivalent experience.
2–4 years of relevant Power BI development experience
Strong hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI, including:
Data modelling (star schema / dimensional modelling)
DAX and Power Query
Performance optimisation and semantic model design
Solid SQL skills and experience working with relational data sources.


Strong bonus


Experience with Microsoft data platforms, such as:
Microsoft Fabric
Azure SQL / SQL Server
Dataflows, pipelines, or equivalent Microsoft-based ETL/ELT tooling

Nice to have / Plus


Experience with SAP BI technologies (SAP BW on HANA, LSA++).
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) experience.



Personal Competencies


Analytical thinker with a sharp eye for data accuracy and consistency.
Structured problem solver who turns complexity into clear, maintainable solutions.
Self-starter who thrives equally when working independently or collaborating with business and IT stakeholders.
Strong communicator: you make data understandable for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Customer-focused and proactive — you anticipate issues rather than waiting for them.
Fluent in English; knowledge of additional languages is a plus.



Additional Information

Unsolicited representations by third parties (recruitment agencies, headhunters, …) of CVs via mail and/or telephone for our vacancies are considered as direct applications where no compensation is provided to the third party. Any T&Cs from these third parties will not be accepted unless upon signature of the T&Cs by a person in charge of HR. Candidates remain registered in the system for 12 months and cannot be proposed again during this period.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Analyst!




Responsabilities:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team. Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals. It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




Requirements:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields.
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI.
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas).
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI.
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas).
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus.
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus.
Good level of English.




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataAnalyst".




Your next challenge might start here.

Join us! 😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!



We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst;

Seniority: Mid-Level / Senior (+3 years);

Type of position: Hybrid (Porto);


The amazing you, will have:

• Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields;

• Professional experience in Data Analysis;

• Strong experience with Python (Pandas);

• Experience with SQL and data manipulation;

• Professional experience with Tableau and/or Power BI;

• Experience with Power Query and DAX;

• Knowledge of Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto or Airflow;

• Experience creating dashboards, reports and data visualizations;

• Ability to analyse, synthesize and simplify complex information;

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills;

• Experience gathering business requirements and translating them into technical solutions;

• Ability to communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders;

• Fluent level of English;


Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Innovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Offering both public and private charging points, EVIO ensures accessibility via an easy-to-use global app.

The platform enables users to share and monetize their chargers in locations such as companies, condominiums, and private garages, complemented by power management and other digital services.

EVIO is committed to making electric vehicle charging seamless, efficient, and accessible.

Title: Pricing Analyst (Atuariado) | Vida (m/f/d)
HTML:
Principais responsabilidades:

Integrado na equipa de Pricing & Business Analytics Vida, esta pessoa terá um papel central na definição e evolução das estratégias de pricing, contribuindo diretamente para a rentabilidade e crescimento do negócio, através de análise de dados e desenvolvimento de modelos analíticos, nomeadamente:


  • Desenvolver e otimizar modelos de pricing de produtos Vida, garantindo o equilíbrio entre risco, competitividade e rentabilidade;
  • Analisar, tratar e explorar dados para suporte à tomada de decisão e evolução da oferta;
  • Avaliar o impacto de alterações tarifárias na carteira atual e no negócio potencial;
  • Aplicar técnicas de modelização estatística, atuarial e de data analytics;
  • Desenvolver, testar e validar modelos, assegurando a sua robustez e aplicabilidade em contexto real;
  • Trabalhar em articulação com diferentes áreas (Produto, Underwriting, Comercial, IT), garantindo uma visão integrada;
  • Participar no desenvolvimento de novos modelos e abordagens de pricing, no âmbito de um programa de transformação em curso.


Procuramos profissionais com o seguinte perfil:


  • Formação superior em Matemática, Estatística, Ciências Atuariais, Engenharia, Economia ou áreas similares;
  • Experiência de 3 a 5 anos em funções analíticas (pricing, atuariado, data analytics ou data science);
  • Interesse claro por pricing, análise de dados e decisão baseada em dados;
  • Experiência em modelização de risco e/ou pricing, idealmente no setor segurador (valorizado, não obrigatório);
  • Capacidade de transformar dados em insights acionáveis para o negócio;
  • Espírito crítico, autonomia e boa capacidade de trabalho em equipa;
  • Conhecimentos de ferramentas como SAS, SQL, Python ou R;
  • Experiência com ferramentas de pricing (ex.: WTW Radar) será valorizada;
  • Fluência em Português e conhecimentos de Inglês.



Como deves efetuar a tua candidatura:

És a pessoa de quem estamos à procura? Se sim, candidata-te clicando em “Efetuar candidatura agora”, até ao próximo dia 22 de junho de 2026.

Title: Data analyst & dashboard designer
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side


Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau
Use Dataiku and PostgreSQL for advanced data processing and statistical analysis
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover hidden correlations and generate additional insights
Continuously improve data models to enhance accuracy, usability and performance


Run Side


Ensure data accuracy, consistency and availability across dashboards
Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes


This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
ETL tools and data modeling best practices
Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
Basic Python skills
Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)


Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills


English: C1


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Excellent communication skills in English (French is a plus)
Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybjrid (50%)

Range: €39,000–€52,000 (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data / Cybersecurity / IT Risk

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data Analyst

This is your mission

Are you passionate about transforming data into actionable insights? Do you enjoy working closely with business stakeholders and building analytical solutions that drive decision-making? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst, you will work at the intersection of business and technology, helping transform complex data into meaningful insights, dashboards, reports, and automation solutions. You will collaborate directly with business stakeholders to understand their challenges, identify opportunities, and deliver data-driven solutions that support strategic decision-making.

Build Side


Gather and analyze business requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Extract, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple data sources.
Design and develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools.
Build automated reporting solutions to improve business efficiency and decision-making.
Develop data analysis workflows using Python and SQL.
Contribute to the industrialization of data feeds, ETL processes, and data quality initiatives.
Create data models and analytical frameworks to generate business insights.
Partner with technical teams to deliver scalable and sustainable analytics solutions.


Run Side


Maintain and enhance existing dashboards, reports, and analytical tools.
Monitor data quality and ensure the reliability of reporting solutions.
Support business users by providing analysis and recommendations based on data findings.
Present analytical results and insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Title: Procurement Data and Insight Analyst
Description:
Description

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst plays a key role in supporting Unilabs’ procurement operations through data analysis, reporting, and actionable insights. This role requires a strong understanding of procurement processes, data analysis methods, and business intelligence tools. The successful candidate will work closely with the procurement team to optimize strategies, identify savings opportunities, and monitor supplier performance.




About the role

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst is responsible for building and maintaining analytical tools and intelligence systems that support procurement planning and operations across Europe. This is primarily a technical role focused on dashboards, automated reporting, and monitoring frameworks that help the procurement team act proactively on contract risks, savings opportunities, and process bottlenecks.

The successful candidate will work at the intersection of data engineering, procurement strategy, and operational excellence, translating complex spend data into clear, actionable insights for senior stakeholders.




Key responsibilities

Tool development & BI ownership core focus

Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards and automated reports to support procurement planning and decision-making
Develop and manage monitoring frameworks that generate real-time alerts for contract expirations, process delays, and sourcing gaps
Build and maintain Excel-based tools using automation features such as Power Query or VBA for ad hoc analysis and modelling
Ensure data quality, refresh reliability, and clear documentation across all developed tools




Procurement intelligence & monitoring core focus

Identify spend categories not covered by active contracts and prioritize them for negotiation cycles
Maintain a live view of the contract portfolio, including renewal timelines, savings tracking, and compliance status
Flag delayed procurement processes and escalate them to the relevant stakeholders in a timely manner
Produce regular spend analyses to identify trends, maverick buying, and cost-saving opportunities across categories




Data analysis & reporting

Collect, structure, and interpret large procurement datasets to identify patterns and improvement opportunities
Define and maintain KPIs, SLAs, and performance benchmarks across the procurement function
Provide ad hoc analysis and reporting to support data-driven decisions by the leadership team




Supplier performance & strategy

Monitor supplier performance against defined KPIs and SLAs, identify gaps, and recommend corrective actions
Conduct market research and supplier analysis to support category strategies and sourcing decisions
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including finance, operations, and legal, to align procurement insights with business priorities




Process improvement

Identify opportunities to streamline procurement workflows and improve operational efficiency
Support the implementation of process standardization initiatives across markets

Requirements




Education

Bachelor's degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Finance, Data Analytics, or a related field




Experience

3–5 years of experience in procurement, data analysis, or business intelligence roles
Proven experience building and owning dashboards and reporting tools; consumption-only experience is not sufficient
Experience in the healthcare, pharmaceutical, or diagnostics industry is a strong advantage




Technical skills

Hands-on experience developing in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and Power Query, is required
Advanced Excel skills, including Power Query and VBA automation, are required
Experience with Tableau or equivalent BI tools is a plus
Experience with Coupa or similar procurement/P2P platforms (e.g., SAP Ariba, Jaggaer) is required
Ability to build automated reporting pipelines and alert mechanisms from structured procurement data
Familiarity with SQL or Python for data extraction and transformation is a plus




Skills & competencies

Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills, with the ability to work with large and complex datasets
Clear and direct communication skills, with the ability to translate data into executive-level insights
High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage competing priorities
Collaborative and cross-functional mindset, with the ability to work effectively across finance, operations, and legal teams




Languages

Fluency in English is mandatory, as it is the working language of the team
Proficiency in Portuguese, French, Spanish, or another European language is a strong advantage given Unilabs’ pan-European footprint

Title: Analista de dados
HTML:
O FC Porto está à procura de um(a) Analista de Dados orientado(a) para resultados, com gosto pela análise, interpretação e transformação de dados em conhecimento aplicável:


Principais Responsabilidades

  • Recolha, tratamento e análise de dados provenientes de várias fontes;
  • Desenvolvimento de dashboards e relatórios em Power BI;
  • Automatizar processos de análise e reporting;
  • Apoio às equipas internas na interpretação de resultados e indicadores;
  • Identificação de padrões, tendências e oportunidades de melhoria;
  • Contribuir para a tomada de decisão estratégica através de análises claras, rigorosas e orientadas para resultados.


Perfil Pretendido

  • Formação em Estatística, Gestão, Economia, Matemática, Engenharia ou área similar;
  • Domínio de Power BI e capacidade de criação de relatórios interativos;
  • Conhecimentos de R, SQL e Google Analitycs (preferencialmente aplicado à análise, tratamento e visualização de dados);
  • Excel avançado (tabelas dinâmicas, fórmulas complexas, Power Query, etc.);
  • Forte sensibilidade para análise exploratória e preditiva de dados, assim como atenção ao detalhe;
  • Espírito crítico, curiosidade intelectual e orientação para a melhoria contínua;
  • Boa capacidade de comunicação e gosto pelo trabalho colaborativo em equipa.

Title: Tableau Developer
Description:
Who are we ?

Quanteam, founding entity of RAINBOW PARTNERS, is a consulting firm specialized in Banking and Financial Services. With more than 1200 consultants across 10 countries, we combine business and IT expertise to support leading institutions in transformation projects.

Our core areas:

➡Quantitative Finance

➡Risk & Compliance

➡Operations & Finance

➡Transformation & Organization

➡ Information Systems.




🔎 We are looking for a Tableau Developer.




📝 MAIN TASKS

Design and develop interactive Tableau dashboards and reports that provide actionable insights.
Ensure dashboards are user-friendly, visually appealing, and aligned with business requirements.
Collaborate with data engineers and analysts to ensure data accuracy, integrity, and availability.
Work with large datasets, perform data cleansing, and optimize data queries for performance.
Engage with stakeholders to understand their data needs and provide solutions that meet their objectives.
Conduct training sessions and workshops to educate users on how to effectively use Tableau dashboards.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to Tableau dashboards and reports.
Continuously monitor and improve the performance of existing dashboards.
Maintain detailed documentation of all dashboards, reports, and data sources.
Keep up to date with the latest Tableau features and best practices.




🧩 YOUR PROFILE AND SKILLS

Academic background in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Science, or a related field.
Professional experience as a Tableau Developer (at least 2 years).
Proficiency in Tableau Desktop and Tableau Server.
Strong SQL skills and experience with databases (e.g., MS SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle).
Professional experience with Python (mainly Pandas).
Professional experience with SQL and ETL processes.
Optimization of Data processes (efficient queries and memory management practices).
Professional experience with Data Analysis or Data Engineering.
Tableau/PowerBI or other visualization tools CI / CD (Jenkins).
Knowledge and interest in financial markets are a plus.




🎁 WHAT WE OFFER

This role offers a gross annual salary between 20.000€ to 31.000€, defined according to the candidate’s experience and competencies.
Permanent contract (CTI).
Meal allowance.
Health Insurance.
Extra holiday: Christmas Eve or New Year’s Eve.
Access to Quanteam Academy for continuous learning and certifications.
Elevate Program to support professional development and career growth.
Career progression plan.
Ongoing follow-up and technical support provided by a Consultant Expert.
Working on a hybrid model.

You have very good communication skills ideally combined with experience in change management

Title: Distributed Cloud | Power BI Specialist
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are seeking a sharp and insightful Power BI Specialist to drive our Business Intelligence initiatives. You will be instrumental in translating complex business requirements into clear data models and compelling visualizations, ensuring strategic decision-making across the organization.

Key Responsibilities:


Design and develop dynamic dashboards and reports using Power BI to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
Gather and translate business requirements into technical specifications for data modeling and reporting solutions.
Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and prepare data for analysis.
Perform data validation and collaborate with stakeholders to ensure data integrity and quality.
Drive the adoption of self-service BI best practices across the business.



Qualifications


3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst or BI Developer.
Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating data.
Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI for data modeling (DAX) and visualization.
Proven ability to successfully elicit and document business requirements.
Language Requirement: Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Digital Solutions & Data Engineer
Description:
At Aptiv, we’re driving the future of mobility — safely, sustainably, and intelligently. We are looking for a highly motivated and versatile professional to join our IT and Automation team, focusing on the development of digital solutions, systems integration, and support for digital transformation in operations

Please submit your application in English

YOUR ROLE


Design and development of applications and workflows using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, and Dataflows for SharePoint).
Configuration and maintenance of Linux servers, including essential services such as HTTP, SMB, PHP, FTP, and other web interface components.
Development and automation of ETL and ELT pipelines for data integration and transformation.
Programming in Python (preferred), PHP, HTML, and C#.
Management and optimization of relational databases (MySQL/MariaDB).
Support and execution of Data Analytics projects, enabling data-driven decision-making.
Participation in Machine Learning and AI initiatives (optional but valued).


YOUR BACKROUND


Bachelors/Masters Degree in Data Engineering or Analytics or equivalent
Proven experience in Data Engineering or Data Analytics.
Solid knowledge of Power Platform and SharePoint integration.
Solid experience in Linux environments.
Experience with data pipeline automation.
Proficiency in multiple programming languages.
Familiarity with relational database systems.
Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively.
Problem-solving mindset and proactive attitude.


WHY JOIN US?


You can grow at Aptiv. Aptiv provides an inclusive work environment where all individuals can grow and develop, regardless of gender, ethnicity or beliefs.
You can have an impact. Safety is a core Aptiv value; we want a safer world for us and our children, one with: Zero fatalities, Zero injuries, Zero accidents.
You have support. We ensure you have the resources and support you need to take care of your family and your physical and mental health with a competitive health insurance package.


YOUR BENEFITS AT APTIV:


Competitive compensation package
Learning, professional growth and development in a world recognized international environment
Access to internal & external training
Recognition for innovation and excellence.
Opportunities to give back to the community
Meaningful work that makes a difference in the world


Please note that to be considered for this position, candidates must be legally eligible to work in Portugal. This includes having:


A valid residence permit or working visa
A Portuguese Tax Identification Number (NIF)
A Portuguese Social Security Number (NISS)


APPLY TODAY, AND TOGETHER LET’S CHANGE TOMORROW!

Title: Digital Analytics Specialist
Description:
Position Snapshot

Location: Braga/Lisbon (Portugal)

Company: Nestlé Business Solutions (NBS)

Full-time/Hybrid Work: 2 days per week at the office (aligned with our current Policy)

Languages: Fluent in English

About Us

Nestlé Business Solutions (NBS) is at the heart of the Nestlé Group. We provide Supply Chain, Financial, HR, Digital & Social Media, CRM and Consumer Engagement services to enable Nestlé to engage with millions of consumers worldwide. We are a multicultural and diverse team of professionals. We are passionate about digital innovation and driving continuous improvement processes with colleagues across the world.

We are looking for talents who are eager to learn, team player, digitally-savvy, with an entrepreneurial mindset, and who can flourish in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

If this describes you and you are looking to join a business where you can really add value, it is time to consider joining NBS!

At Nestlé Business Solutions (NBS), diversity and inclusion are core values. If you have any special needs, count on us to adapt your position.

Position Summary

For our Nestlé Business Services, we are currently looking for a Digital Analytics Specialist. A regular working day in this position would involve collaborating with marketing and IT teams to gather tracking requirements for new campaigns and initiatives, implementing and testing tracking codes in Google Tag Manager to ensure accurate data collection and reporting, and monitoring and maintaining existing tracking codes, troubleshooting issues, and optimizing data collection processes for improved insights and reporting.

A day in the life of...


Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics administration
Implementing and maintaining tags: Ensuring accurate implementation and maintenance of tags across digital platforms using Google Tag Manager.
Collaboration with cross-functional teams: Working with marketing, IT, and analytics teams to gather tracking requirements for new campaigns and initiatives.
Data analysis and interpretation: Analyzing data collected through Google Tag Manager to provide insights and make informed decisions for optimization and marketing strategies.
Troubleshooting and issue resolution: Identifying and resolving tracking issues, ensuring data accuracy, and optimizing data collection processes for improved insights and reporting.
Training and documentation: Providing training to teams on Google Tag Manager best practices, documenting tracking processes, and ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations.


What Will Make You Successful


University Degree ideally specialized in Marketing, Computer Science or any other related fields
+2 years of experience in Digital Technologies and Data & Analytics fields (focused in Website and Mobile Apps analytics)
Proficiency in Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
4- Familiar with HTML, JavaScript, and data layer architecture to effectively manage and troubleshoot tracking codes
Knowledge of GDPR and data privacy regulations as they pertain to digital analytics
Understanding of using data visualisation tools (Power BI, Looker Studio), product analytics dashboarding is a plus
Data warehouse and cloud solutions basic understanding (GCP, BigQuery, Azure)
Familiarity with A/B testing, conversion rate optimization, and user experience analysis to drive improvements based on the data collected through Google Tag Manager.
Project Management skills and knowledge of agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban)
High mathematical/analytical skills
Understanding marketing processes
High communication skills and coordination of multiple stakeholders in complex and fast changing environments


What We Offer 

Here at NBS we care about our Talents and we continuously work to have our teams engaged and our people achieving their best professional performance, without forgetting personal development and work-life balance. Joining us will bring you:


Development of expertise in Nestlé’s Shared Solutions Business
Immersion in a culturally diverse team
Local and international exposure
Flexible working environment
Engagement and wellbeing activities
Competitive salary and annual bonus according to your performance
Meal allowance card
Flex Benefits - at NBS you can choose what benefits are more suitable for you
Make part of the Nestlé Club and get discount in several partners
Free coffee (and good coffee) at the office
Shop with special discounts for employees
Company equipment according to professional needs
Medical support available at the office
E-learning courses and training program to get you where you aim to be
Career progression and possibilities for international career

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About The Job

In today's digital landscape, data is the cornerstone of success. Sofico recognizes this truth and is assembling a brand-new team to help harness the potential of data within the Miles Enterprise Suite.

As a Data Engineer at Sofico, you will play a crucial role in unlocking the full potential of the data ecosystem within Miles Enterprise. Your mission is to extract actionable insights from the rich data resources, catering to both on-platform and off-platform reporting needs.

Key Responsibilities:


Efficient extraction of data from the specialized internal data models of Miles Enterprise
Transformation of raw data into formats optimized for reporting and analysis
Provision of robust data management capabilities, including data entry and adjustments, validation, reconciliation, versioning, enrichment, and mapping
Provision of a solid infrastructure for security, auditability, orchestration, scheduling, monitoring, client extensibility and customization
Development of outbound data consumption interfaces for seamless integration with customers' existing data warehouses (or lakes) and BI platforms
Creation of a reference data warehouse c.q. purpose-specific data marts within Miles Enterprise to extend on-platform reporting capabilities
Design and implementation of various data visualizations, including enterprise reports, self-service BI tools, dashboards, and KPI metrics



By becoming a part of Sofico's forward-thinking data engineering team, you will be at the forefront of revolutionizing the automotive finance industry. Collaborating with passionate team members, you will redefine the boundaries of data utilization, shaping the future of our industry through your contributions.

Having established a solid foundation, you will have the chance to venture into more advanced topics such as predictive modeling, data mining, real-time data streaming, or integration with LLMs. Your role will continuously evolve, presenting new opportunities for professional development and innovation.

Your Interests And Skills

Your analytical skills and critical mindset will be important when dealing with data model, pipeline, and architectural complexity.

Attention to detail is also crucial for ensuring data accuracy, quality, and consistency.

You will work as a scrum team with the product owner to deliver on the platform requirements.

To thrive in this role, you’ll need:


3-5 years’ experience in a relevant data engineering role
Experience with PostgresSQL, Python and Java
Excellent communication skills in English, with fluency in Dutch considered a plus
You are comfortable working in scrum mode, your potential to act as a Scrum Master is a plus



What we offer you

We Offer


A competitive salary paid 14 times a year
Full medical insurance covered by Sofico
daily meal allowance for every day worked
Flexibility with adjustable hours and work-from-home options
A sleek, modern office with excellent accessibility and amenities
Tailored six-month onboarding with your own job coach
Exciting team events: think after-work drinks, BBQs, and more

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
At LUXCLUSIF, we are dedicated to redefining the luxury paradigm by offering premium pre-owned fashion to a global audience. As a pioneer in the global luxury resale market, we utilize advanced data-driven solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of high-end pre-owned items. With operations spread across the globe and strategic warehouses in key locations, we ensure a seamless supply of luxury goods. Our comprehensive services cover everything from item curation, quality control, operations, logistics to technology integrations and sales, delivering an unmatched resale experience for brands, retailers, and individual customers alike.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
As a Senior Data Analyst, you will join our Product Analytics team to champion data best practices and strengthen our technical foundations. You will deliver impactful insights regarding our tech products while playing a pivotal role in evolving our self-service data environment. By partnering closely with our Data Engineering team and power users across verticals, you will centralize business logic, optimize our data models, and ensure our analytics products remain performant, scalable, and governed.

What You'll Do


Centralize Business Logic: Identify decentralized logic (such as complex cost calculations currently living inside Power BI semantic models) and push it upstream into centralized, well-documented data models
Empower Self-Service: Provide our vertical power users with a clean, highly performant, and governed data foundation so they can confidently build the reports they need independently
Champion Data Governance: Drive data governance efforts across our growing self-service ecosystem, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the company
Mentor & Elevate: Act as technical anchor for our team, mentoring on SQL optimization, dimensional modeling, and BI best practices
Deliver Product Analytics: Maintain and evolve core company reports while providing deep-dive analytics on our tech products to support strategic business decisions





Who You Are


Experienced Professional: You have 5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Analytics Engineering, or Business Intelligence, with a strong track record of improving data infrastructure and performance
Tech Savvy: You possess expert-level SQL skills and extensive experience with Power BI (including DAX optimization and semantic models) and cloud data platforms like Azure (particularly Databricks). Ideally, you also bring hands-on experience with dbt and Google BigQuery as we plan to evolve our data stack
Architectural Thinker: You can easily identify bottlenecks in data pipelines or decentralized logic, and you take the initiative to design and implement efficient, scalable solutions
Collaborative Mentor: You are a team player who enjoys collaborating closely with data team members and stakeholders

Title: Senior Data Analyst - MarAnalytics
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

MARKETING
The Marketing team is data-driven and results-oriented, focused on achieving measurable outcomes across all digital advertising and marketing channels. Growth is a key performance indicator, and they constantly optimize for return on ad spend.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
This is an exciting role for a skilled and experienced Senior Data Analyst to drive data-driven decision-making across the organization. This role will focus on experimentation, measurement, and delivering actionable insights to optimize performance and enhance business outcomes.

What You'll Do


Design, execute, and optimize experiments across paid marketing channels, leveraging data-driven methodologies to increase ROAS.
Continuously refine experimentation frameworks to identify growth opportunities and enhance performance.
Proactively identify opportunities to automate reporting processes, improve efficiency, and reduce manual effort.
Integrate and analyse data from diverse internal and external sources to uncover acquisition, retention, and optimization opportunities for paid marketing channels.
Provide actionable recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness.
Effectively communicate insights, analyses, and recommendations to stakeholders at all levels, from marketing teams to senior leadership.
Build and implement statistical models to forecast demand, measure sales incrementality, and assess the impact of key marketing initiatives.
Leverage advanced analytical techniques to enhance decision-making and drive strategy.
Stay updated on industry trends, new analytical tools, and marketing technologies.
Apply this knowledge to improve existing processes, adopt innovative solutions, and maintain a competitive edge





Who Are You


A professional with 3+ years of experience in a data role, preferably in an internet company;
Strong analytical and quantitative skills; experience using data and metrics to test theories, confirm assumptions, and measure success;
Expert in SQL. BigQuery experience is a plus;
Familiar with data visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau, Google Analytics, Data Studio, PowerBI or Microstrategy;
Degree educated. A quantitative field is considered a bonus;
Advanced Excel knowledge;
Advanced in English, both written and oral;
Excellent written and oral communication;
Using R or Python for statistical modelling is a plus;
Experience in Digital Marketing and Retention Analytics is a plus;
Curious about learning and applying data science to solve business problems





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities and free access to Udemy
Flexible benefits program
Flexible work environment- Hybrid Model (3 days a week from the office, 2 days from home)

Title: Power BI & Dataiku Developer/Mid-Level Data Engineer
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We would like to meet a Power BI & Dataiku Developer/Mid-Level Data Engineer for a project with a hybrid working model in Porto, Portugal.




Responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards and reports aligned with business needs.
Build efficient data models in Power BI (star schema, measures, KPIs, DAX best practices).
Perform data preparation and feature engineering using Dataiku (visual recipes and code-based workflows).
Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure clean, reliable, and analytics-ready datasets.
Contribute to data science workflows in Dataiku, including exploratory data analysis and model experimentation.
Support Generative AI use cases (e.g. prompt-based analytics, LLM integration, AI-assisted insights) within Dataiku.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and usability across reports and analytical assets.
Participate in documentation, best practices, and knowledge sharing within the team.




Requirements:

3–6 years of experience in BI, Analytics, or Data-related roles.
Hands-on experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, and visualization best practices).
Hands-on experience with Dataiku for data preparation and analytics workflows.
Solid understanding of data visualization principles and storytelling with data.
Experience preparing datasets for analytics and machine learning use cases.
Basic to intermediate knowledge of data science concepts (e.g. statistics, ML fundamentals).
Familiarity with Python and/or SQL.
Fluent in English (written and spoken).




Nice to Have

Experience building or supporting machine learning models in Dataiku.
Exposure to Generative AI or LLM-based use cases (e.g. embeddings, prompt engineering).
Experience with cloud data platforms.
Understanding of data governance and model lifecycle management.







Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability




Join us

Send us your CV in English to mariana.azevedo@decskill.com, with reference "MA/DATBI".

Your next challenge might start here.







At Decskill, we are committed to equal opportunities and non-discrimination. We promote a culture of diversity and inclusion, where recruitment and career progression are based solely on talent, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality, or any other form of discrimination incompatible with human dignity.
